title: Blocking DNS resolution inside async validate_url
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/web.rs:153-202 (validate_url) called from src/web.rs:263 (fetch_url, async)
## Violation
`validate_url` invokes `(host, port).to_socket_addrs()` (line 185-188), which is the standard-library *blocking* DNS resolver. It is called synchronously from inside `pub async fn fetch_url`. Under tokio this blocks the executor thread for the entire DNS lookup; a slow or hanging resolver stalls every other task on that worker.

```rust
let socket_addrs: Vec<_> = (host, port)
    .to_socket_addrs()
    .map_err(|e| format!("Cannot resolve host '{host}': {e}"))?
    .collect();
```

This is also called from `execute_web_fetch` via `block_on(web::fetch_url(...))`, so the blocking call happens *inside* an `async` context driven by `block_in_place`.
## Mandated refactor
Move the DNS lookup off the runtime: wrap in `tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || (host, port).to_socket_addrs())` and `.await` it, or use `tokio::net::lookup_host`. Better, install a custom `reqwest::dns::Resolve` that performs the lookup and the forbidden-range check atomically at dial time (kills both this issue and the redirect bypass).
## Filed from QA review dispatch
